questions.question: When is A2A the right protocol instead of MCP?
options: Whenever the agents are written in different languages
When two opaque agents must collaborate — delegating a Task while each keeps its internal reasoning private; MCP is agent-to-tool, A2A is agent-to-agent
When tools need OAuth
A2A replaces MCP for all new projects
questions.explanation: MCP calls tools; A2A lets a customer-service agent hand a Task to a specialist writer agent across frameworks, seeing only state transitions and outputs. The two protocols are complementary, not competing.

questions.question: How does one agent discover another's capabilities in A2A?
options: A central registry assigns capabilities
Fetch the Agent Card at /.well-known/agent.json — it lists skills with input/output modes, the A2A endpoint URL, and capabilities like streaming
Send a probe message and parse the reply
Capabilities are hard-coded in the SDK
questions.explanation: Discovery is URL-based: the card names the agent, its skills (e.g. summarize_paper with text/file input modes), endpoint, and capability flags. The AP2 extension adds JWT signatures so cards can't be impersonated.

questions.question: Which A2A task lifecycle path is valid?
options: completed → working → submitted
submitted → working → input_required → working → completed
rejected → working
working → submitted
questions.explanation: Tasks flow submitted → working, may loop through input_required (the called agent needs more from the caller), and end in a terminal state: completed, failed, canceled, or rejected. Clients follow along via SSE subscription or polling.

questions.question: What are the three Part types an A2A message can carry?
options: header, body, footer
text (plain content), file (base64 blob with mimeType), and data (typed JSON payload)
prompt, completion, embedding
task, message, artifact
questions.explanation: One message can combine all three — e.g. a text instruction, the paper.pdf file, and a data part with {targetLength: "3 paragraphs"} as structured input. Artifacts are the called agent's outputs.

questions.question: What is A2A's governance and adoption status as of v1.0?
options: A Google-proprietary API in closed beta
Released by Google (April 2025), donated to the Linux Foundation, v1.0 in April 2026 with 150+ supporters; it absorbed IBM's ACP and added the AP2 payments extension
An academic proposal with no implementations
Merged into the MCP specification
questions.explanation: A2A followed the same neutral-governance path as MCP: vendor-launched, foundation-donated, broadly backed (AWS, Cisco, Microsoft, Salesforce, SAP, ServiceNow among supporters).
